source-discipline.reason: The 11-DR roster was established by grep over decisions/ for PROPOSED status markers (not from memory or prior summaries); each Group-B summary paragraph was written after reading the DR's own header and problem statement this session; Group-C items C1-C4 restate owner-blockers already recorded in workplan/website-v0-path-forward-2026-07-12.md and the 2026-07-12 inventory audit, cited rather than re-derived; the drift findings (PopulationCode enum vs populations table, bpc_metadata NDV-for-deaf, 27/640 and 0/640 substrate coverage) were each verified by direct DB query this session.

bias_direction: The package recommends 'ratify' for 10 of 11 items, and its author authored or executed several of them - a systematic pressure toward ratification framing. Bounded by: every recommendation is one owner reply from being overridden; the package explicitly offers 'deferred' as a first-class outcome (B5 is recommended either way); Group B items are proposals that have been operating de-facto since May, so ratify-or-defer genuinely is the decision space, and the one item with an unverified precondition (B4, migration 015 state) says so instead of smoothing it over.
independent_reviewer_counterclaim: A reviewer might argue that bundling 11 decisions into one sitting pressures wholesale approval and that the 'demonstration to look at first' framing primes acceptance. Response: each item is separately answerable by design, the A5 sub-items are individually addressable, and the pilot demonstration pointed to is the one artifact whose checks an owner can re-run mechanically rather than take on trust.
